acceptance_criteria: No persisted artifact or served dashboard response contains raw API keys, common token patterns, private key blocks, configured secret values, raw command text, raw environment values, unsafe absolute paths, omission placeholders, raw artifact bodies, raw manifests, raw diff bodies, validation output payloads, planner handoff payloads, or attacker-controlled denied path payloads.
All docs, run artifact, manifest, log, planner, Codex event, diff, eval, validation, and dashboard-visible writes are redacted before persistence.
Traversal attempts using relative, absolute, encoded, or symlinked paths cannot read or write outside the configured workspace or allowed run artifact roots.
jj run accepts only plan files that resolve inside the configured workspace after documented path resolution, canonicalization, and symlink evaluation.

External, traversal, absolute-escape, encoded-escape, symlink-escape, unreadable, malformed, and secret-looking plan inputs are rejected before content read or artifact persistence with deterministic sanitized diagnostics.
In-workspace relative, process-cwd-resolved, and absolute in-workspace plan paths continue to work in dry-run and non-dry-run modes.
Plan input and Codex artifact symlink swaps are rejected without reading, persisting, or rendering out-of-root content.
Low-information configured sensitive literals such as true, false, null, and none do not become global redaction values.
jj serve displays intended docs and run artifacts through guarded paths only, including dashboard, detail, history, comparison, and audit export routes.
Duplicate hostile query values and secret-looking or token-like run IDs are denied deterministically without reflecting rejected values.
Command records include only sanitized argv, status, timing, cwd label, and result metadata and do not persist raw command text or raw environment dumps.
Persisted manifests and general JSON artifacts use safe path labels and do not store raw workspace, plan, external config, git, repository, validation command, or baseline paths.

Git baseline, git diff, and validation stdout and stderr artifacts are sanitized before persistence and expose redaction evidence only as booleans, counts, sanitized categories, and safe relative labels.

Manifest and dashboard security diagnostics expose only counts, booleans, root labels, relative-safe roots, sanitization status, dry-run parity status, sanitized failure categories, sanitized validation output redaction evidence, and sanitized git diff redaction evidence.

Dry-run and non-dry-run produce equivalent redaction, boundary, diagnostic, validation output, planner handoff metadata, git diff redaction behavior, artifact persistence, and safe path label persistence.

Planner request, Codex fallback prompt, agent handoff JSON, planning context snapshot, task proposal reason, event, manifest, and log fields use guarded sanitizer helpers and do not persist unsafe raw payloads.

Missing, malformed, partial, older, denied, stale, tampered, or internally inconsistent run metadata renders deterministic denied, unknown, unavailable, or sanitized states on detail, history, compare, and audit export surfaces without server errors or unsafe fallback fields.

Run detail, run history, run comparison, and audit export handlers obtain run metadata through shared sanitized DTO or helper paths and do not perform handler-local raw manifest, artifact, command, environment, diff, or filesystem reads after route input parsing.

Focused Go regression tests cover redaction, path guards, artifact writing, manifest scrubbing, command metadata, dry-run and non-dry-run parity, diagnostics, serve access, run inspection access, Codex artifact symlink rejection, low-information literal filtering, secret-looking run ID denial, guarded plan input handling, sanitized validation output handling, git diff redaction evidence, planner and Codex handoff sanitization, duplicate hostile query denial, and needs_work terminal status.

Auto or balanced mode resolves to bugfix only for explicit positive current evidence and does not resolve to bugfix for healthy or negated evidence such as passed validation, passed tests, failed_count zero, no blocker, all tasks done, or no runnable tasks.

Auto or balanced mode resolves to security only for concrete release-gate, CI, validation, test, disclosure, or boundary evidence and does not resolve to security from healthy release-gate evidence, completed security guardrail text, plan.md background vision, or absent evidence.
No new runnable task is opened from healthy or absent evidence.
Security remains the selected category for any future concrete regression involving secrets, paths, artifacts, commands, manifests, validation output, planner handoff, or dashboard exposure.
Any future security task cites concrete failing evidence from scripts/validate.sh, focused tests, full tests, vet, build, diff-check, CI, confirmed disclosure, or boundary evidence.
Future regression work is scoped only to the confirmed failing behavior and includes focused recurrence coverage.
No new dashboard pages, CLI flags, raw exports, scanners, artifact uploads, or unrelated user-facing features are recommended as part of healthy release-gate follow-up work.
scripts/validate.sh is documented as the single local and CI release validation entry point and runs the focused security boundary suites, full tests, vet, CLI build, and diff-check.
The release validation entry point and CI wrapper suppress child output and print only deterministic release-gate metadata with fixed labels, categories, booleans, counts, and exit codes.

CI invokes scripts/validate.sh for security-relevant pull request or push changes, fails on non-zero exit, and does not enable continue-on-error, verbose shell tracing, child output passthrough, raw artifact upload, raw manifest upload, raw diff upload, secret echoing, or environment dumps.

Focused regression coverage proves the release validation workflow has exactly one scripts/validate.sh run step, preserves security-relevant triggers, and denies continue-on-error, verbose shell tracing, artifact upload, environment dump, raw manifest, raw diff, and secret-reference constructs.
No completed task is reopened unless scripts/validate.sh, focused Go tests, full tests, vet, build, diff-check, or CI inspection finds a concrete security regression.

A narrow CI release-gate privacy verification may inspect CI, documentation, release-gate behavior, and focused regression coverage, but must patch only concrete gaps and must not add new dashboard pages, CLI flags, raw export endpoints, scanners, artifact upload behavior, or unrelated user-facing features.
TASK-0039 validation passes through the sanitized release validation entry point.

TASK-0040 verification runs scripts/validate.sh as the only release-gate command, passes with sanitized release-gate metadata, applies no implementation patch, and leaves completed security guardrails closed.
TASK-0041 task proposal guardrail validation passes focused internal run tests and scripts/validate.sh with sanitized release-gate metadata, and completed security guardrails remain closed.
goals: Generate concrete specs and tasks from a single plan file while preserving an auditable run history.

Keep generated artifacts, served files, plan inputs, workspace mutations, validation outputs, git diff artifacts, planner and Codex handoff metadata, and run inspection surfaces inside the resolved workspace or guarded run artifact roots.

Prevent secrets, token-like values, private key material, omission placeholders, raw commands, raw environment values, unsafe paths, raw manifests, raw artifact bodies, raw diff bodies, validation output payloads, planner handoff payloads, and attacker-controlled denied payloads from being persisted or rendered.
Provide a dashboard-first local jj serve experience with guarded run detail, history, comparison, and sanitized audit export views.
Preserve dry-run and non-dry-run workflows with identical redaction, path-boundary, diagnostics, artifact persistence, diff redaction, validation, and planner handoff metadata behavior.
Ensure auto and balanced task proposal resolution selects bugfix only from positive current blocker evidence and does not loop on healthy, negated, or completed-task context.

Ensure auto and balanced task proposal resolution selects security only from concrete current release-gate, CI, validation, test, disclosure, or boundary evidence and not from completed guardrail policy or background vision alone.
Enforce plan input path handling so explicitly provided plans cannot escape the configured workspace or leak external sensitive content.
Persist git diff artifacts only after redaction and expose any diff redaction evidence through sanitized metadata only.
Treat stale, partial, malformed, manually edited, or internally inconsistent run metadata as untrusted unless fields are sanitized through guarded DTO helpers.
Use scripts/validate.sh as the single documented release validation entry point for local and CI security release gates.
Keep completed security guardrails closed unless a concrete release-gate, CI, validation, test, disclosure, or boundary regression is observed.
non_goals: Provide a multi-user dashboard or authentication system.
Replace git review or guarantee AI output correctness.
Add unrelated user-facing features while unresolved or residual security risks are being verified.

Expose raw commands, environment dumps, secret values, sensitive absolute paths, private key material, token-like strings, omission placeholders, raw artifact bodies, raw manifests, raw diff bodies, validation output payloads, planner handoff payloads, raw prompt exports, or attacker-controlled denied path payloads.
Add raw manifest export, raw artifact export, raw diff export, raw prompt export, artifact content bundling, raw artifact upload, raw manifest upload, or raw diff upload.

Add new dashboard pages, CLI flags, raw export endpoints, broad file import behavior, scanners, redaction algorithm expansion, or unrelated user-facing behavior as part of security validation or CI release-gate follow-up work.
Allow explicitly provided plan files outside the workspace by default.
Reopen completed tasks without fresh failing validation, tests, CI, or confirmed disclosure or boundary regression evidence.
Expose child command stdout or stderr from the release validation entry point or CI wrapper.
requirements: jj run must read a non-empty Markdown plan from the explicitly provided path using documented path resolution behavior, then require the resolved plan file to remain inside the configured workspace boundary.
The resolved cwd value, or process cwd when cwd is absent, is the workspace boundary for generated docs, run artifacts, workspace mutations, and accepted plan input files.

Plan path validation must canonicalize requested paths, reject unsupported encoded traversal and escape forms, evaluate symlinks where applicable, reject traversal attempts, and reject symlink or absolute-path escapes outside the workspace before reading content.
Plan input files must be revalidated through the same guarded path result immediately before readback so symlink replacement cannot cause out-of-workspace reads or persistence.

Rejected external, traversal, absolute-escape, encoded-escape, symlink-escape, unreadable, malformed, or secret-looking plan inputs must produce deterministic sanitized errors without reflecting raw paths, raw OS errors, secrets, token-like values, omission placeholders, or attacker-controlled payloads.
In-workspace relative plans, process-cwd-resolved plans, and absolute in-workspace plans must continue to work in dry-run and non-dry-run modes after canonicalization and symlink evaluation.

Accepted plan content, planner output, Codex output, Codex summaries, command summaries, evaluation output, validation output, docs writes, manifest serialization, event writes, run artifact writes, git diff artifacts, and dashboard render data must pass through shared redaction before persistence or display.

Manifest and general JSON persistence must use safe workspace, run, relative, or generic path labels and must not store raw workspace, plan, external config, git, repository, validation command, or baseline paths.
Command execution records must persist sanitized argv, cwd labels, status, duration, and result metadata only; raw command text and raw environment values must not be stored or rendered.

Planner requests, Codex fallback prompts, agent handoff JSON, planning context snapshots, task proposal reasons, events, manifests, logs, and planner/fallback metadata must be sanitized before persistence, logging, rendering, or manifest inclusion.

Prompt and context builders must not directly concatenate raw manifests, command output, validation payloads, diff bodies, environment values, denied path inputs, or unsafe artifact bodies into persisted handoff metadata.

Validation stdout and stderr artifacts and git diff artifacts must be sanitized before persistence; evidence reports may include only booleans, counts, sanitized category labels, and safe relative artifact labels.

Dry-run and non-dry-run modes must apply identical plan path validation, redaction, path-boundary, artifact persistence, git diff capture, diff redaction, manifest metadata, security diagnostics, validation output sanitization, planner handoff metadata sanitization, and denial behavior.

jj serve must only serve files from guarded workspace and run artifact roots and must reject traversal, absolute, encoded, secret-looking, token-like, duplicate hostile query, or symlink escape inputs with deterministic denied or unavailable states.
Run IDs may contain only safe letters, numbers, dots, underscores, and dashes, must not use traversal-like forms, and must be rejected when they match configured secrets or common token patterns.
Web run completion handling must treat needs_work as a terminal status.
Codex event and summary outputs must resolve under the guarded run artifact root before launch and must be revalidated through the artifact store before fallback creation, redaction, or readback.
Configured sensitive literals used for redaction must ignore low-information values such as true, false, null, and none.

Run detail, run history, run comparison, and run audit export handlers must obtain metadata through shared sanitized DTO construction or clearly factored shared helpers, not handler-local raw manifest, artifact, command, environment, diff, or filesystem reads after route input parsing.

Untrusted, stale, partial, malformed, manually edited, or internally inconsistent manifest fields, artifact references, validation summaries, command metadata, diff metadata, provider fields, timestamps, and next-action hints must render only deterministic denied, unknown, unavailable, or sanitized states.

Auto or balanced task proposal resolution must choose bugfix only from positive current evidence of failed validation, failed tests, failed or blocked runnable tasks, provider failure, panic, fatal error, or regression; healthy or negated evidence must not trigger bugfix resolution.

Auto or balanced task proposal resolution must choose security only from concrete release-gate, CI, validation, test, disclosure, or boundary evidence showing a security or privacy regression; healthy release-gate context, completed security guardrail text, plan.md background vision, and absent evidence must not open or reopen security work.
Recent security evidence used for task proposal selection must be reduced to sanitized category labels before influencing future task selection.
Structured SPEC guardrail text must not resolve to hardening, quality, or documentation debt modes by keyword alone when no current actionable evidence exists.
Future security regression tasks must cite exact failing evidence and remain narrowly scoped to the confirmed behavior, with regression coverage for recurrence.

scripts/validate.sh must remain the single documented release validation entry point, run the focused security boundary suites, full tests, vet, CLI build, and diff-check, suppress child command output, emit only deterministic sanitized metadata, and exit non-zero on any failing step.

CI must invoke scripts/validate.sh for security-relevant pull request or push changes, fail on non-zero exit, and must not enable verbose shell tracing, child output passthrough, raw artifact upload, raw manifest upload, raw diff upload, secret echoing, environment dumps, or continue-on-error for the release gate.

Focused regression coverage must verify the release validation workflow invokes exactly one scripts/validate.sh run step for security-relevant triggers and rejects privacy-sensitive constructs including continue-on-error, shell tracing, artifact uploads, environment dumps, raw manifest or diff references, and secret references.
Existing release validation documentation must state that scripts/validate.sh is both the local and CI release gate.

Completed security guardrails must remain closed unless scripts/validate.sh, focused Go security suites, full tests, vet, build, diff-check, or the CI release gate produces a concrete disclosure, path-boundary, artifact, dashboard, planner handoff, validation output, git diff, command metadata, or release-gate output regression.
summary: jj is a CLI that turns a planning file into implementation-ready specs, tasks, Codex runs, validation artifacts, and a local dashboard. Security remains the controlling constraint: generated artifacts, served run-inspection surfaces, explicit plan inputs, command metadata, manifests, validation outputs, git diff artifacts, planner and Codex handoff metadata, and run metadata must be guarded, redacted, and kept inside the configured workspace or allowed run artifact roots. jj serve includes guarded dashboard, run detail, history, comparison, and sanitized audit export flows. TASK-0036 through TASK-0040 established and verified scripts/validate.sh as the single sanitized local and CI release gate. TASK-0041 tightened auto and balanced task proposal resolution so security work is selected only from concrete release-gate, CI, validation, test, disclosure, or boundary evidence; healthy release-gate context, completed guardrail text, and plan.md background vision no longer reopen security or debt work by themselves. Recent security evidence used for future selection is reduced to sanitized category labels. Focused internal run tests and scripts/validate.sh passed with sanitized release-gate metadata. Completed security guardrails remain closed unless concrete validation, test, CI, disclosure, or boundary evidence proves a regression.
title: jj Security Boundary and Dashboard Run Inspection
